title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
summary: Sandipan Bhaumik outlines a five-pillar production AI deployment framework: evaluation (numerical success metrics before implementation), observability (tracing all agent decisions for regulatory compliance), data foundation (quality requirements for agents), multi-agent orchestration, and governance (security testing for PII). The approach emerged from a retail bank engagement where six weeks of infrastructure work proved critical before model selection and deployment.
writeup: Sandipan Bhaumik of Databricks lays out a production playbook for deploying AI agents at enterprise scale, structured around five pillars: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk grounds the framework in a retail bank engagement, where tracing infrastructure surfaced that a stale knowledge base drove customer satisfaction drops following a policy update. The takeaway: instrumenting agents for evaluation and observability catches failures that data and governance gaps would otherwise hide.
